content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer AAA. Let's define an infinite sequence S(A)=A%P,A2%P,A3%P,…S(A) = A\%P, A^2\%P, A^3\%P, \ldotsS(A)=A%P,A2%P,A3%P,…, where P=108+7P = 10^8+7P=108+7 is a prime and %\%% is the modulo operator.

Let's also define a decreasing sum D(S)D(S)D(S) as the sum of all elements of a sequence SSS which are strictly smaller than all preceding elements of SSS. When SSS is a sequence of non-negative integers, the number of such elements is clearly finite.

Find D(S(A))D(S(A))D(S(A)).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer AAA.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer D(S(A))D(S(A))D(S(A)).
